items.title: Why younger generations are worse off than baby boomers

children.content: According to research from the Actuaries Institute, inequality between the younger and older generations is widening and could reach record levels within years.

gallery.altText: Family of six walk along beach on a sunny day.

thumbnail.altText: Family of six walk along beach on a sunny day.

items.title: Auction clearance rates at 2022 housing downturn levels, ASX climbs

children.content: Auction clearance rates have fallen to levels not seen since the 2022 housing downturn. The ASX is up solidly as oil prices drop on hopes of a Middle East peace deal. Follow live.

image.altText: An A-frame sign that reads "auction today", directing toward a house.

items.title: Major winemaker threatens to move business overseas if tax changes go through

children.content: The managing director of Taylors Wines in South Australia is threatening to move the business internationally in response to the federal government's proposed tax changes.

image.altText: Two people in flurescent vests walking through a vineyard

items.title: KPMG boss quits amid auditing scandal

children.content: KPMG Australia chief executive Andrew Yates has stepped down amid a scandal involving audit partners accessing confidential client documents.

image.altText: A bald man in a suit and red tie speaks into a microphone while siting at a desk in a room decorated with timber.

items.title: Australians have 'startlingly' low awareness of controllable dementia risk factors

children.content: Too many Australians still see dementia as an inevitability, according to new data, but there is a list of steps you can take to reduce the risks.

image.altText: Jane Turner sits at a desk.

items.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad during test

children.content: Blue Origin says it has experienced an "anomaly", a term commonly used by rocket companies to describe a launch failure or explosion.

items.title: A 'horror night' of airstrikes leaves city wondering what's next

children.content: New waves of missile strikes and vast advisories from the Israeli government to evacuate have left residents in Lebanon's south uncertain of where to turn.

image.altText: A car covered in debris from an airstrike in a residential area.

items.title: White House asks Americans to report their neighbours as 'aliens'

children.content: The website features a space-inspired background but its real purpose is to highlight the work of ICE.

image.altText: a screenshot of a white house website that reads "they walk among us"

items.title: James Valentine remembered as 'wonderful human being' at public memorial

children.content: The veteran broadcaster's family, friends and loyal listeners gathered in Sydney Town Hall to pay tribute to the 64-year-old who died last month, two years after being diagnosed with cancer.

gallery.altText: Man in a suit jacket and pants wears glasses, smiling, while holding a saxophone

thumbnail.altText: Man in a suit jacket and pants wears glasses, smiling, while holding a saxophone

items.title: Erin Patterson's appeal against mushroom murder conviction set for August

children.content: Erin Patterson's appeal against her triple murder conviction will be heard in August, Victoria's Supreme Court has confirmed, with the director of public prosecutions also challenging the length of her jail term.

image.altText: Erin Patterson being led into a prison van

items.title: Trump appointees push for $250 note featuring US president's face

children.content: The decision will be made by the US House and Senate, but reports say Donald Trump's supporters are pushing to expedite the process.

image.altText: Donald Trump listens during a cabinet meeting at the White House.

items.title: What the fight over London data centre plans tells us about the AI backlash

children.content: Last year in the US alone, projects collectively worth $200 billion were scuppered or delayed as communities protested the construction of new data centres promised to deliver the AI of the future.

gallery.altText: A man in a vampire outfit lies on the steps of an office building with protest signs against data centres behind him.
A protester with a sign reading 'Stop the AI Race' walking among other demonstrators down a city street.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a vampire outfit lies on the steps of an office building with protest signs against data centres behind him.
A protester with a sign reading 'Stop the AI Race' walking among other demonstrators down a city street.

avatar.alt: A man smiling at the camera.

items.title: Kiwi goalkeeper gets bride's blessing to ditch wedding for World Cup

children.content: Auckland FC goalkeeper Michael Woud has had to put his wedding plans on hold after being selected in New Zealand's squad for the World Cup, but he says taking part in soccer's biggest tournament is not a bad reason to cancel.

image.altText: Goal keeper crouching near the goal post, gesturing to his teammates to move to the right in defence

items.title: Farnham says he will not sing on stage again ahead of tribute concert

children.content: Jimmy Barnes, Tina Arena and Human Nature will take the stage to honour Australian legend John Farnham at a Melbourne tribute concert later this year.

image.altText: John Farnham.

items.title: This comedian has 1.7m followers. He doesn't think it's real

children.content: As a comedian with legions of fans on social media, Will Gibb wishes he didn't still feel the need to legitimise himself. But he does.

gallery.altText: Will Gibb holds a finger to his mouth and gently smiles wearing an orange and maroon crochet shirt against orange backdrop.

thumbnail.altText: Will Gibb holds a finger to his mouth and gently smiles wearing an orange and maroon crochet shirt against orange backdrop.

items.title: 'Disappointing': Man loses home deposit over feud with developer

children.content: It was lauded as a solution to the housing crisis, but those involved say plans to build an "eco village" in northern New South Wales have gone awry.

image.altText: Serge Killingbeck wears a hat with his arms crossed behind a garden.

items.title: Our critics can't stop listening to these 10 albums from May

children.content: From long-awaited comebacks to First Nations glam rock and genre-defying polymaths, May was stacked with excellent new releases.

image.altText: Collage of Genesis Owusu, Ecca Vandal, Kneecap and Mulga Bore Hard Rock.

items.title: Heatwaves unbearable in UK 'built for a climate that no longer exists'

children.content: As the mercury climbed around the United Kingdom this week and new May maximums were recorded in multiple areas, the once-welcome sunshine is sparking alarm among many people.

gallery.altText: A man lies in the sun, with a large bridge visible behind him.
Several people lying on towels on dry grass, seen from above.

thumbnail.altText: A man lies in the sun, with a large bridge visible behind him.
Several people lying on towels on dry grass, seen from above.

avatar.alt: A woman smiling at the camera.

items.title: Queensland prisoner loses appeal to freeze eggs

children.content: The Court of Appeal knocked back the woman's attempt to have her eggs frozen while she serves a ten year prison sentence.

image.altText: Gloved hands hold a tube over a canister which has mist coming out of it.

items.title: Two taken into custody as police enter protest camp at Olympic stadium site

children.content: Tensions have flared at Victoria Park after police, firefighters and Brisbane City Council officers entered a protest camp ahead of the $3.6 billion Olympic stadium development.

image.altText: Council workers standing around a tent

items.title: How tax rules benefit top earners by $700,000 but median earners by $5,700

children.content: Commonwealth Treasury secretary Jenny Wilkinson says the average top income earner benefited from the existing investment tax arrangements to the tune of $700,000 over their lifetime, versus just $5,700 for median income earners.

image.altText: Treasurer Jim Chalmers and Treasury Secretary Jenny Wilkinson in treasurer's office

items.title: Government defends blocking counterterror documents from Bondi royal commission

children.content: The federal government has made a public interest immunity claim over the documents before the Royal Commission into Antisemitism and Social Cohesion, essentially arguing it is not in the public interest for the documents to be released.

image.altText: A woman with dark hair, wearing a lilac jacket, standing next to a man in a suit.

items.title: Eels reveal Moses injury outlook ahead of Origin II

children.content: Parramatta do not have any concerns with the handling of Mitchell Moses's fitness in the NSW Blues set-up, as the Eels skipper faces a race against the clock to play before Game II of State of Origin.

image.altText: Rugby league player in a blue suit walking through the passage way inside a stadium

items.title: Newly discovered ancient wasp species named after F1 driver Piastri

children.content: Biologists have named the wasp species
Gwesped piastrii
, honouring Australian Formula 1 driver Oscar Piastri.

image.altText: F1 driver sitting during a press conference, smiling, listening to a question

items.title: Refugee who tried to flee the Taliban 15 times finally safe to play cricket

children.content: After the Taliban banned women's sport in Afghanistan, most of the women's cricket team ended up relocating to Australia. Now, they're on tour in the Northern Territory — "a happy ending" after a traumatic journey.

gallery.altText: Three women in view, one in centre mid-speech, wearing a pink jersey, black hijab, all standing on a green sports field.
A sole women's cricket player, Afghan, hot-pink long-sleeve shirt, blue pants, knee pads and gloves on, standing on pitch.
High-angle shot of huddle of Afghan women cricket players, hot-pink shirts, blue caps, standing on green sports field.

thumbnail.altText: Three women in view, one in centre mid-speech, wearing a pink jersey, black hijab, all standing on a green sports field.
A sole women's cricket player, Afghan, hot-pink long-sleeve shirt, blue pants, knee pads and gloves on, standing on pitch.
High-angle shot of huddle of Afghan women cricket players, hot-pink shirts, blue caps, standing on green sports field.

items.title: Amazon sued over alleged button battery safety failure in kids backpacks

children.content: The consumer watchdog is suing Amazon alleging it sold children's unicorn backpacks on its online marketplace that failed to comply with mandatory button battery safety warnings.

image.altText: A pink backpack with a white, pink, blue and yellow unicorn.

items.title: Insect repellent may not always keep mozzies away, study suggests

children.content: Scientists have found that mosquitoes in the lab can be trained to connect the smell of a widely used repellent with food, but repellents are still the best way to protect yourself.

image.altText: Close up photo of a mosquito on a green leaf.

items.title: Drag queen says Patagonia has launched trademark suit, claiming 'irreparable harm'

children.content: A drag queen and a major outdoor clothing brand have a fight over trademarks. Here's the feud explained.

image.altText: A drag queen with long hair poses in the woods.

items.title: Philosopher tasked with keeping Australians safe from AI

children.content: A philosopher and Royal Australian Air Force reservist has been chosen to lead the federal government's efforts to keep Australians safe from the harms of AI.

image.altText: A young white woman standing in front of a military plane

items.title: After escaping sexual slavery, Yazidis re-traumatised by return of 'ISIS brides'

children.content: The return of several 'ISIS brides' from Syria has been re-traumatising for Australian Yazidis, many of whom escaped sexual slavery at the hands of the terrorist organisation.

gallery.altText: A woman looks over the Shingal region, Iraq

thumbnail.altText: A woman looks over the Shingal region, Iraq

items.title: Investigators unable to find why plane engine failed due to fire damage

children.content: The pilot in his 60s suffered a spinal injury in the crash last year, as well as burns to 80 per cent of his body.

image.altText: A destroyed aeroplane

items.title: Officeworks to offshore hundreds of jobs in search for white-collar workers

children.content: The Australian office products retailer is planning to offshore hundreds of jobs to India and the Philippines, joining a growing trend of businesses hoping to find more highly skilled white-collar workers overseas.

image.altText: A large outdoor Officeworks sign in front of large trees.

items.title: Second NSW government minister suspended over secret documents

children.content: Government frontbenchers John Graham and Penny Sharpe have been suspended from the upper house over failing to produce documents demanded by the opposition.

image.altText: Composite side by side of John Graham and Penny Sharpe

items.title: Loopholes in laws make crackdown on illegal vapes difficult

children.content: More than 20 million illegal vapes, with a street value of just over $1 billion, have been seized by law enforcement authorities since Australia's vaping reforms came into effect.

image.altText: Vape products in a gloved hand.

items.title: Kids protest against McDonald's after fast-food giant buys land in small town

children.content: Children turn out in protest after it's revealed the global fast-food giant has paid $4.6 million for a prominent site in central Victoria's Castlemaine.
